description: useRef(createSupabaseBrowserClient()) was called from game-client.tsx and host-controls.tsx. useRef's argument runs on EVERY render — paired with the 250ms useCountdown timer, this constructed ~12 fresh GoTrueClient instances per second and piled up thousands of auth listeners against the same storage key, eventually crashing Chrome. Issue showed up as 'Multiple GoTrueClient instances detected' warning incrementing in the console.
resolution: Both factories in lib/supabase/client.ts now cache at module scope (cachedDataClient, cachedAuthClient). Repeated calls return the same instance. PR #82 shipped this fix.

content: Supabase splits long auth tokens into chunked cookies: sb-<ref>-auth-token.0, .1, .2, etc. with a 'base64-' prefix. The manual cookie reader in lib/supabase/client.ts concatenates and base64-decodes. Don't replace it with a naive single-cookie read or you'll break sessions for big JWTs (paid Supabase tiers / federated providers).

Use vanilla @supabase/supabase-js for data + realtime; use @supabase/ssr ONLY for auth flows (sign-in card, user menu, callback). The ssr browser client has a getSession() hang that stalls every data call. Two factories in lib/supabase/client.ts: createSupabaseBrowserClient (data, vanilla) and createSupabaseAuthBrowserClient (auth, ssr).
